Research on AI governance is documented almost entirely through positive artifacts. Standards bodies publish standards; agencies publish guidance; labs publish benchmarks and model cards; projects publish frameworks and audit templates. These artifacts are col

Nekropolis assembles that negative record into one place and one schema. It is a *negative-results corpus*: a curated dataset of public artifacts whose lifecycle ended in retraction, withdrawal, revocation, archival, or discontinuation, across AI research and

The contribution is not the list of retractions. Retraction Watch already maintains a far larger retraction database, and that database is one of the inputs here. What Nekropolis adds is **cross-domain unification**: journal retractions, preprint withdrawals,

**Methods.** A 13,502-record AI/ML slice of Retraction Watch (Nekropolis corpus v0.2) is analyzed for cause distribution, tier composition, and temporal pattern. Each record carries a precision tier — title-level, method-adjacent, venue or metadata, broad comp

**Conclusions.** Across both the title-level population (3,243) and the inclusive AI/ML population (13,502), computer-generated content is the dominant documented cause. The integrity system has responded through retraction rather than prevention at submission
**Keywords:** retractions; research integrity; Retraction Watch; artificial intelligence; machine learning; computer-generated content; paper mills; peer review.

# Computer-generated content and the AI/ML retraction record, 2018–2026: a characterization study

**Background.** Retraction Watch is the canonical public record of retracted scientific publications. Its AI/ML-related share has grown sharply since 2022, when large language models made fluent scientific prose cheap to produce. That domain has not been chara

**Methods.** A 13,502-record AI/ML slice of Retraction Watch (Nekropolis corpus v0.2) is analyzed for cause distribution, tier composition, and temporal pattern. Each record carries a precision tier — title-level, method-adjacent, venue or metadata, broad comp

**Conclusions.** Across both the title-level population (3,243) and the inclusive AI/ML population (13,502), computer-generated content is the dominant documented cause. The integrity system has responded through retraction rather than prevention at submission
**Keywords:** retractions; research integrity; Retraction Watch; artificial intelligence; machine learning; computer-generated content; paper mills; peer review.
